mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0 percent, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that increases corporate profit margins and capital expenditure cycles, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of co-occurring instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rising industrial production index reflects capacity utilization normalization and supply chain restoration, which expands earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months during identified recovery regimes, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in regime-filtered backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances indicate expanding household purchasing power and consumption demand, which directly supports quarterly revenue guidance and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive skew when RSAFS increases month-over-month, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% across all market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase exceeding 0.50 percentage points within a single policy meeting compresses equity risk premiums and raises borrowing costs for leveraged firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.50 percentage points in one month
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a 0.50pp rate hike, or the forward return distribution shows no significant negative skew relative to untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent rises in the unemployment rate and declines in real GDP indicate deteriorating economic fundamentals that erode corporate earnings and trigger risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of co-occurring instances, or the cross-product term's predictive power vanishes when controlling for volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, a sustained drop in housing starts signals impending consumer demand weakness and inventory buildup, which compresses forward earnings multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or neutral when HOUST drops below 1.2 million units during identified expansion phases, or the signal's directional accuracy fails to exceed 55% in walk-forward testing.
